content.problemStatement:
There are NNN shops in the town, numbered from 111 to NNN. The iii-th shop has an infinite amount of items with value ViV_iVi‚Äã each. And each item in each shop costs exactly 111 dollar.

To prevent you from spending all your money buying just the items of maximum value, you have to follow some rules:



Let numinum_inumi‚Äã be the number of items you bought at the iii-th store.




num1num_1num1‚Äã can only be either 000 or 111.




numi‚â§numi‚àí1+1num_i \leq num_{i-1}+1numi‚Äã‚â§numi‚àí1‚Äã+1 for 2‚â§i‚â§N2\leq i \leq N2‚â§i‚â§N.



Find the maximum sum of value you can obtain if you can spend a total of KKK dollars.


Input Format

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the number of shops and the money you have.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,V2,V3,...,VNV_1, V_2, V_3, ... , V_NV1‚Äã,V2‚Äã,V3‚Äã,...,VN‚Äã - the value of the items in the NNN shops.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer - the maximum sum of value you can achive.
constraints: **Subtask 2 (19 points):** 1‚â§N,K‚â§5001 \leq N, K \leq 5001‚â§N,K‚â§500, **Time limits = 2s in this Subtask**.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The optimal choice is to buy 111 item in the first shop, 222 in the second, 111 in the third, 000 in the fourth, and 111 in the fifth.
The sum of values are (1√ó3)+(2√ó5)+(1√ó4)+(0√ó2)+(1√ó6)=23.(1 \times 3) + (2 \times 5) + (1 \times 4) + (0 \times 2) + (1 \times 6) = 23.(1√ó3)+(2√ó5)+(1√ó4)+(0√ó2)+(1√ó6)=23.
